sub_problems: How should an agent runtime detect a local micro-failure (parsing error, retry event, edge-case mishandling) precisely enough to trigger local repair without globally disrupting the rest of behavior?

What pre-positioned 'reserve' material — cached alternative responses, fallback heuristics, latent skill modules — should be carried in dispersed form throughout the runtime so that failures can expose and recruit it on demand?
How does the exposed reserve crystallize into a permanent local refinement at the failure site, and how does the refinement avoid spreading beyond the locality it was triggered for?
Over many cycles of failure-and-repair, how is reserve depletion measured and replenished, and what is the failure mode when reserves are exhausted?
